summary: One-line matching fix in fighter dynamics: replaced an `HSD_ASSERT(364, 0)` fallback in `ftCo_8009D81C` with an explicit `__assert(__FILE__, 364, "fp->dynamics_num < Ft_Dynamics_NumMax")`. The PR body says the incorrect assert was causing a lower match on the function.

what_changed.change: Changed the assert used after detecting `fp->dynamics_num >= Ft_Dynamics_NumMax` from `HSD_ASSERT(364, 0)` to `__assert(__FILE__, 364, "fp->dynamics_num < Ft_Dynamics_NumMax")`.
Kept the preceding `OSReport("fighter dynamics num over!\n")` behavior unchanged.

smart_moves: Used the exact failed condition string, `fp->dynamics_num < Ft_Dynamics_NumMax`, instead of asserting a constant false expression. This better reflects source-level assert semantics and improved matching according to the PR body.
Made a minimal targeted change: one deleted line and one added line, avoiding unrelated fighter dynamics edits.
decomp_lessons: When an assert is known to represent a violated condition, matching may require spelling the original condition in `__assert` rather than using a macro form like `HSD_ASSERT(line, 0)`.
For asserts following an explicit range check, preserve the source condition text if evidence suggests the compiled binary includes that condition string.
Small assert-string and macro-vs-raw-assert differences can affect match quality even when runtime control flow appears equivalent.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_8009D81C` follows the project convention for currently address-named fighter common functions.
`Ft_Dynamics_NumMax` is used as the fighter dynamics upper-bound constant.
`fp->dynamics_num` and `data->hat_dynamics[3]->dynamicsNum` show the mixed field naming present in this area; the PR did not rename them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Prefer direct `__assert(__FILE__, line, "condition")` when the binary appears to reference the textual assertion condition.
Do not assume `HSD_ASSERT(line, 0)` is equivalent for matching; macro expansion and string literal selection can matter.
Assert line numbers should remain exact; this change preserved line 364.
follow_up_queries: Find other uses of `HSD_ASSERT(line, 0)` where the original source condition may be recoverable and improve matching.
Search fighter dynamics functions for asserts that may need explicit `__assert(__FILE__, line, "condition")` forms.
Compare match output for `ftCo_8009D81C` before and after this assert-string correction.
